d9dea9d1-c8bc-4800-9b0d-87976f19de03.text: Distortion titled 'Catastrophizing' with description 'Expecting the worst possible outcome without considering other possibilities.'. Examples of this distortion are '[If I make a mistake, I'll definitely get fired., This headache must be a brain tumor.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What's the most likely outcome?, How have similar situations turned out in the past?]' .

a7691911-92ff-4b58-a827-fb03e181c5e5.text: Distortion titled 'Emotional Reasoning' with description 'Believing that because you feel a certain way, it must be the objective truth.'. Examples of this distortion are '[I feel overwhelmed, so my problems must be impossible to solve., I feel like a bad friend, so I must be one.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Am I confusing a feeling with a fact?, What objective evidence contradicts how I am feeling right now?]' .

a45c143e-c321-4628-9ce1-993c79d6c72b.text: Crisis Protocol titled 'De-escalation Techniques' is described as 'Using verbal and non-verbal strategies to calm a highly agitated or distressed individual.' .Examples of this technique are '[Speaking in a slow, calm, and steady voice while maintaining non-threatening body language., Actively listening and validating their feelings without arguing or trying to 'fix' it immediately.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Is my tone and body language communicating safety and calmness?, How can I validate their distress without necessarily agreeing with their thoughts?]' .

c73fcc81-3d08-43c7-b705-f8bd11548a03.text: Distortion titled 'Mental Filtering' with description 'Focusing exclusively on the negative details of a situation while ignoring the positive.'. Examples of this distortion are '[My boss gave me a great review but mentioned one area for improvement, so I must be terrible at my job., I ruined the whole party because I spilled my drink.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What positive details am I ignoring in this situation?, If a friend were looking at this, what good things would they notice?]' .

c4e0aad5-6b54-470f-b873-e71afd25b684.text: Cbt Technique titled 'Problem-Solving Therapy' is described as 'A structured method for identifying problems, brainstorming solutions, and implementing action plans.' .Examples of this technique are '[Breaking down a massive financial debt into small, actionable monthly budgeting steps., Creating a pros-and-cons list to decide whether to change careers.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What is the specific, solvable problem I am facing right now?, What are three possible solutions, regardless of how silly they might seem at first?]' .

98ad1eb0-c9a1-41d7-807a-4c30492b2076.text: Burnout Content titled 'Work-Life Balance Techniques' with description 'Methods for creating a sustainable equilibrium between professional demands and personal life.'. Examples of this distortion are '[Leaving work equipment at the office to prevent working from home., Designating specific 'no-screen' hours during the evening to focus on family.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What does a healthy balance between my work and personal life actually look like?, Are my current daily habits supporting my personal life, or eroding it?]' .

2c741548-523b-408a-82f0-2df96ace6642.text: Distortion titled 'Fortune Telling' with description 'Predicting that things will turn out badly without any realistic evidence.'. Examples of this distortion are '[I just know I'm going to fail this exam., There is no point in trying because they will reject me.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Can I really predict the future with 100% accuracy?, What is an alternative, more positive way this could play out?]' .

73e0cee3-db69-48e2-a715-f796902c02ba.text: Distortion titled 'Mind Reading' with description 'Assuming you know what others are thinking without evidence.'. Examples of this distortion are '[They must think I'm incompetent., She didn't smile because she hates me.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What evidence do you have for this assumption?, Could there be other explanations for their behavior?]' .

2939cf2b-f8a9-4362-864e-e0916dc75a40.text: Crisis Protocol titled 'Emergency Resources' is described as 'Identifying and utilizing professional services designated for immediate crisis response and intervention.' .Examples of this technique are '[Calling the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline., Going to the nearest hospital emergency department or calling 911.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What is the fastest way to get professional, immediate help right now?, Do we have the correct phone numbers and addresses readily available for an emergency?]' .

c2c52283-3bea-4ce6-910b-873787c36537.text: Distortion titled 'Should Statements' with description 'Using rigid rules about how you or others should behave.'. Examples of this distortion are '[I should always be productive., They should know how I feel.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Where does this rule come from?, What would happen if you replaced 'should' with 'prefer'?]' .

8234ea83-b588-401f-b731-dc3691a87933.text: Distortion titled 'Labeling' with description 'Assigning global, negative traits to yourself or others based on a single behavior.'. Examples of this distortion are '[I made a mistake, therefore I am a loser., He cut me off in traffic, he is a complete monster.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Is this label a fair summary of the whole person?, Can I describe the specific behavior instead of using a global label?]' .

beb60ce4-d026-48c9-980a-596ab19b2cc6.text: Burnout Content titled 'Boundary Setting' with description 'Communicating and enforcing clear limits regarding time, energy, and responsibilities to protect well-being.'. Examples of this distortion are '[Politely declining to take on a new project when already at capacity., Setting an auto-responder stating you do not check emails after 6:00 PM.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What is a reasonable limit I need to set to protect my mental health?, How can I communicate my boundaries clearly but respectfully to my team?]' .

8c8c5f1e-783a-4ec5-a4b8-6ec13f37d592.text: Crisis Protocol titled 'Safety Planning Steps' is described as 'Collaboratively creating a concrete, step-by-step plan to keep the individual safe during periods of intense distress.' .Examples of this technique are '[Writing down a list of coping strategies and people to call when feeling overwhelmed., Removing access to lethal means or unsafe environments.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What are the concrete steps we can put in writing to ensure safety tonight?, Who are three specific people or organizations we can list to contact in an emergency?]' .

ac001a0b-2ee5-45c3-851f-ed2f0337a044.text: Cbt Technique titled 'Relaxation and Mindfulness' is described as 'Using physiological techniques and present-moment awareness to reduce physical arousal and psychological distress.' .Examples of this technique are '[Practicing progressive muscle relaxation before bed., Doing a 5-minute deep breathing exercise to center oneself during a stressful workday.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Where am I holding physical tension in my body right now?, Can I gently bring my attention back to the rhythm of my breath?]' .

0c9a134b-b738-4150-a582-f65dddb49d84.text: Distortion titled 'Personalization' with description 'Taking the blame for events that are not entirely under your control.'. Examples of this distortion are '[It's my fault the project failed, even though the client changed the requirements., My child got a bad grade, I must be a terrible parent.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What other factors contributed to this outcome?, Am I taking responsibility for someone else's choices or random circumstances?]' .

1446ed82-4651-4419-9ac7-21e32d938796.text: Cbt Technique titled 'Exposure Techniques' is described as 'Gradually and systematically facing feared objects, situations, or memories in a safe environment to reduce avoidance and anxiety.' .Examples of this technique are '[Looking at pictures of spiders before progressing to being in the same room as one., Practicing public speaking in front of a mirror before speaking to a small group.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What is the smallest, safest step I can take toward facing this fear?, If the worst-case scenario happened during this exposure, how would I handle it?]' .

c5c8d7b7-f55d-4214-89ca-79ce82d2779b.text: Crisis Protocol titled 'Warning Signs Recognition' is described as 'Identifying behavioral, emotional, or verbal cues that indicate a person is entering a state of crisis or danger.' .Examples of this technique are '[Giving away prized possessions or sudden isolation., Drastic mood swings, sudden agitation, or expressing feelings of hopelessness.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What specific changes in behavior have I noticed that seem out of character?, Are these signs indicating immediate danger that requires urgent escalation?]' .

e050c371-9deb-4372-9d4f-2263f975638d.text: Distortion titled 'All-or-Nothing Thinking' with description 'Seeing things in black-and-white categories with no middle ground.'. Examples of this distortion are '[If I'm not perfect, I'm a total failure., Either I do it right or not at all.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Is there a middle ground between these extremes?, Can you think of shades of gray in this situation?]' .

2a19fb83-607b-4b48-b240-785d15cc3aa7.text: Burnout Content titled 'Recovery Strategies' with description 'Active and passive techniques used to replenish physical and psychological resources depleted by chronic stress.'. Examples of this distortion are '[Taking actual, unplugged vacations without checking work emails., Engaging in restorative hobbies like gardening, painting, or yoga.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What activities actually make me feel recharged rather than just distracted?, How can I proactively schedule recovery time into my weekly routine?]' .

ff7e1dec-1648-4fc3-b5de-00239a790f0c.text: Cbt Technique titled 'Thought Challenging / Cognitive Restructuring' is described as 'Identifying and disputing irrational or maladaptive thoughts to replace them with more balanced alternatives.' .Examples of this technique are '[Using a thought record to analyze the evidence for and against a negative belief., Recognizing a cognitive distortion and actively writing down a more realistic thought.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[What is the evidence that supports or contradicts my current thought?, Is there a more helpful or realistic way to view this situation?]' .

c980f75a-c5d9-4b1d-8ea4-5517e2507e29.text: Distortion titled 'Overgeneralization' with description 'Making broad conclusions based on a single event.'. Examples of this distortion are '[I failed once, so I'll always fail., Nobody ever listens to me.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Is this truly always the case?, Can you think of exceptions to this pattern?]' .

22fde761-6494-40d2-9ea0-8d99c8157e5d.text: Cbt Technique titled 'Behavioral Activation' is described as 'Scheduling and engaging in positive, meaningful, or highly rewarding activities to combat depressive symptoms and lethargy.' .Examples of this technique are '[Scheduling a 15-minute walk every morning., Committing to calling a friend even when feeling unmotivated.]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[How might taking a small, positive action right now shift my mood?, What is one small activity I used to enjoy that I can try for 10 minutes today?]' .

d47e3a5d-76b0-4975-bcc6-91797d67d65f.text: Burnout Content titled 'Maslach Burnout Inventory Dimensions' with description 'The three core dimensions of burnout: Emotional Exhaustion, Depersonalization (cynicism), and Reduced Personal Accomplishment.'. Examples of this distortion are '[Feeling utterly drained before the workday even begins (Exhaustion)., Treating clients or colleagues as impersonal objects rather than people (Depersonalization).]' .Some reframing questions to use are '[Which of these three dimensions is affecting me the most right now?, Am I viewing my colleagues cynically, or am I just deeply exhausted?]' .
